Title: Carotenoid oxygenases: cleave it or leave it

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: crocin is a red pigment; picrocrocin and safranal contribute bitter taste and aroma
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes synthesis of crocin, picrocrocin, and safranal through zeaxanthin cleavage
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning from stylar tissues; sequence comparison with conserved carotenoid cleavage enzymes; expression pattern analysis; inferred catalytic activity from pathway assignment in paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZCD is a novel zeaxanthin cleavage dioxygenase cloned from saffron styles. 2. It catalyzes 7,8 (7',8') cleavage of zeaxanthin leading to crocin, picrocrocin and safranal biosynthesis. 3. Its expression is restricted to stylar tissue, consistent with apocarotenoid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of valuable colorants, flavors, and aromas in crops or microorganisms
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene here because it is a carotenoid-cleaving oxygenase discussed as a specialized apocarotenoid-forming enzyme rather than a central pathway enzyme in a functional engineering study.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative cleavage step of ABA precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate ABA precursor by cleaving 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cleaves the 11,12 double bond of 9-cis-violaxanthin, 9-cis-neoxanthin, and 9-cis-zeaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress response and seed dehydration responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA biosynthesis; cleavage reaction is rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; transposon tagging; recombinant enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. vp14 was cloned from a maize ABA-deficient mutant. 2. Recombinant Vp14 cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids at the 11,12 bond and also accepts 9-cis-zeaxanthin. 3. The enzyme requires a 9-cis double bond adjacent to the cleavage site and controls the rate-limiting step of ABA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Epoxy group is not absolutely required for activity despite NCED nomenclature.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes, supported as rate-limiting step in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered ABA may affect drought/stress responses and seed physiology
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NCED/carotenoid cleavage oxygenase family; homologous to lignostilbene dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modifying ABA levels to improve drought and stress tolerance

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative cleavage step of ABA precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: orthologous enzyme forming ABA precursor by carotenoid cleavage
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Orthologous NCED cleavage of 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA biosynthesis as tomato orthologue of Vp14
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype and orthology to Vp14 as described in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. notabilis is a tomato ABA-deficient mutant. 2. The Not gene product is an orthologue of maize Vp14. 3. It belongs to the NCED class involved in the key carotenoid cleavage step of ABA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence summarized from prior cloning studies in review context.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported by orthology to Vp14 and pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered ABA may affect stress tolerance and development
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune ABA-mediated agronomic traits

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative cleavage step of ABA precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms ABA precursor through carotenoid cleavage
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Orthologous NCED-catalyzed cleavage in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Involved in ABA biosynthetic pathway in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: orthology and functional assignment summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNCED3 is an Arabidopsis orthologue of Vp14. 2. It functions in the ABA biosynthetic pathway. 3. It exemplifies plant NCED family expansion with conserved ABA-related function.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as one of several Arabidopsis NCED family members.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Implied key control step by pathway role
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered ABA can influence drought response and development
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering ABA-associated drought/stress tolerance

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms volatile apocarotenoid products such as β-ionone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves the 9,10 (9',10') double bond of various carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: flower scent; flavor and aroma compound
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of volatile apocarotenoids including β-ionone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A plant enzyme cleaving the 9,10 (9',10') double bond of various carotenoids was characterized. 2. The name CCD was proposed to distinguish it from NCEDs. 3. Its physiological role is uncertain, but β-ionone derivatives are essential scent components in flowers.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact physiological role not fully known according to paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering floral scent and aroma traits

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms retinal for vitamin A biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Centrally cleaves β-carotene at the 15,15' double bond to generate retinal
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: visual chromophore, vitamin function, developmental signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes vitamin A biosynthesis by catalyzing the first carotenoid cleavage step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning and characterization from various animal species; mutant phenotype in Drosophila ninaB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Animal BCO enzymes were cloned and shown to catalyze the first step in vitamin A synthesis. 2. Drosophila ninaB mutation reducing BCO activity lowers retinoid and rhodopsin levels and causes blindness. 3. Animal BCO may operate by a monooxygenase-like mechanism via a transient carotene epoxide.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses mechanism as possibly monooxygenase rather than dioxygenase.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as first step in vitamin A synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: BCO loss causes reduced retinoids and blindness in Drosophila
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports metabolic engineering of retinol production in plants and microorganisms

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: acts in retinoid metabolism rather than direct carotenoid cleavage
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Involved in all-trans- to 11-cis-isomerization of retinal in the visual cycle
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in retinoid metabolism, not direct carotenoid cleavage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression and functional characterization summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RPE65 was the first animal NCED homologue described. 2. It appears to function in retinoid rather than carotenoid metabolism. 3. It is implicated in all-trans to 11-cis retinal isomerization in retinal pigment epithelium.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a central summarized carotenoid oxygenase family member, although not a carotenoid-cleaving enzyme per se.
